question: What section of the 2023 Florida Building Code, Building, governs the width and capacity of corridors used as a means of egress?
gold_answer: Section 1020.3 (Width and Capacity) of Chapter 10 (Means of Egress) governs corridor width and capacity: the required capacity is determined per Section 1005.1, and the minimum width shall be not less than that specified in Table 1020.3 (generally 44 inches, with exceptions). NOTE: Section 1020.2 is 'Construction' (fire-resistance rating), not width.

question: What is the minimum headroom clearance required over a walking surface in the means of egress?
gold_answer: A minimum headroom of 80 inches (2032 mm) must be provided over any walking surface in the means of egress, per Section 1003.3.1 (Headroom) of the Florida Building Code, Building. Section 1003.2 (Ceiling Height) sets the general means-of-egress ceiling height at not less than 7 feet 6 inches.

question: Under what conditions does Section 1006.2.1 of the Florida Building Code, Building, require two exits or exit access doorways from a single space?
gold_answer: Two exits or exit access doorways are required from any space where the design occupant load or the common path of egress travel distance exceeds the values listed in Table 1006.2.1.

question: What is the means-of-egress capacity factor per occupant for components other than stairways in sprinklered buildings with an emergency voice/alarm communication system, per the 2023 FBC?
gold_answer: For other than Group H and I-2 occupancies, the capacity of means of egress components other than stairways is calculated at 0.15 inch per occupant in buildings equipped with an automatic sprinkler system per Section 903.3.1.1 or 903.3.1.2 and an emergency voice/alarm communication system per Section 907.5.2.2.

question: What is the means-of-egress capacity factor per occupant for stairways in sprinklered buildings with an emergency voice/alarm communication system, per the 2023 FBC?
gold_answer: For other than Group H and I-2 occupancies, the capacity of means of egress stairways is calculated at 0.2 inch per occupant in buildings equipped with an automatic sprinkler system per Section 903.3.1.1 or 903.3.1.2 and an emergency voice/alarm communication system per Section 907.5.2.2.

question: What section of the 2023 Florida Building Code, Building, governs wind loads, and which standard does it reference for detailed calculation?
gold_answer: Section 1609 of Chapter 16 designates the minimum wind loads a structure must withstand. The 2023 8th Edition references ASCE 7-22 for detailed calculation (previously ASCE 7-16 in the 2020 7th Edition).

question: How is the ultimate design wind speed (Vult) for Risk Category II buildings determined under the 2023 Florida Building Code, Building?
gold_answer: The ultimate design wind speed Vult for Risk Category II buildings and structures is obtained from Figure 1609.3(1), per Section 1609.3 of the Florida Building Code, Building.

question: Under the 2023 FBC, on which figure is the wind-borne debris region based for Risk Category II buildings?
gold_answer: The wind-borne debris region for Risk Category II buildings and other structures (and Risk Category III except health care facilities) is based on Figure 1609.3(1).

question: What Florida Building Code section governs foundation walls and retaining wall design for lateral soil loads, and where are the minimum design soil loads found?
gold_answer: Section 1610 governs foundation walls and retaining walls designed to resist lateral soil loads. Table 1610.1 provides the minimum design lateral soil loads unless a geotechnical investigation under Section 1803 determines otherwise.

question: What section of the 2023 Florida Building Code, Building, governs geotechnical investigations for foundation design?
gold_answer: Section 1803 of Chapter 18 governs geotechnical investigations for foundation design in the 2023 8th Edition Florida Building Code, Building.

question: What section addresses grading and fill placement requirements in flood hazard areas under the 2023 FBC, Building?
gold_answer: Section 1804.5 (Grading and Fill in Flood Hazard Areas) governs fill placed in flood hazard areas, requiring it be placed, compacted, and sloped to remain stable. Section 1804.4 (Site Grading) covers general grading away from the building, not the flood-hazard-specific fill rule.

question: What Florida Building Code section governs construction seaward of the Coastal Construction Control Line (CCCL)?
gold_answer: Section 3109 sets requirements for construction seaward of the Coastal Construction Control Line, administered jointly with the Florida Department of Environmental Protection.

question: What Florida Building Code section requires elevation certification to be submitted for buildings in flood hazard areas after the foundation inspection?
gold_answer: Section 110.3 requires that in flood hazard areas, upon placement of the lowest floor (including basement), an elevation certification be submitted to the authority having jurisdiction prior to further vertical construction.

question: What section of the Florida Building Code, Residential, addresses flood-resistant construction requirements, including provisions for coastal high-hazard areas?
gold_answer: Section R322 covers flood-resistant construction. R322.3 contains the specific requirements applicable in coastal high-hazard areas.

question: Which Collier County code section governs flood variance requests, including variances from the flood-resistant construction requirements of the Florida Building Code?
gold_section: Collier County Code of Ordinances, Sec. 62-77
gold_answer: Sec. 62-77 (Variances and appeals, Chapter 62, Floods, Art. II) governs flood variances and appeals: the Office of the Hearing Examiner (or Planning Commission) hears variances from ordinance requirements that are not FBC requirements, while pursuant to F.S. 553.73(5) the Board of Building Adjustments and Appeals hears appeals and variances from the flood-resistant construction requirements of the Florida Building Code (excluding FBC Section 3109).

question: Which Collier County ordinance adopted the 2023 8th Edition Florida Building Code, and which subsequent ordinance amended that adoption?
gold_section: Collier County Ordinance 2023-64, amended by Ordinance 2024-40
gold_answer: Collier County Ordinance 2023-64 adopted the 2023 8th Edition Florida Building Code, and was subsequently amended by Ordinance 2024-40.

question: What is the Pre-FIRM cutoff date used by the City of Naples to determine flood insurance rating for existing structures?
gold_section: Collier County / Naples floodplain administration (FEMA/NFIP administrative date, not an FBC section)
gold_answer: The City of Naples uses July 2, 1971 (its initial FIRM date) as its Pre-FIRM cutoff; unincorporated Collier County uses September 14, 1979 — both per the FEMA NFIP Community Status Book (Naples CID 125130C, Collier CID 120067C). This is an administrative FEMA/NFIP date, not a Florida Building Code section citation.

question: How is 'means of egress' defined in Section 202 of the 2023 Florida Building Code, Building?
gold_answer: A means of egress is a continuous and unobstructed path of vertical and horizontal egress travel from any occupied portion of a building or structure to a public way, consisting of three separate and distinct parts: the exit access, the exit, and the exit discharge.

question: What are the three separate and distinct parts of a means of egress under the Florida Building Code?
gold_answer: The three parts of a means of egress are the exit access, the exit, and the exit discharge.

question: What is a 'public way' as defined by the Florida Building Code?
gold_answer: A public way is a street, alley, or other similar parcel of land essentially open to the outside air, deeded, dedicated, or otherwise permanently appropriated to the public for public use and having a clear width and height of not less than 10 feet.

question: What is the minimum required stairway width for a residential stairway under the Florida Building Code, Residential (R311.7.1)?
gold_answer: Residential stairways must have a minimum clear width of 36 inches above the permitted handrail height, with reduced clear-width allowances at and below the handrail projection per R311.7.1.

question: What is the minimum tread depth and maximum riser height for a residential stairway under the Florida Building Code, Residential?
gold_answer: The maximum riser height is 7-3/4 inches and the minimum tread depth is 10 inches, per Sections R311.7.5.1 (Riser height) and R311.7.5.2 (Tread depth) of the Florida Building Code, Residential.

question: What is the maximum vertical rise permitted between landings in a single residential stairway flight?
gold_answer: A stairway flight cannot have a vertical rise greater than 12 feet 7 inches between floor levels or landings, per Section R311.7.3.

question: What Section 1023 requirement applies to interior exit stairways serving floor levels more than 30 feet below the exit discharge level, per the 2023 FBC?
gold_answer: Interior exit stairways serving floor levels more than 30 feet below the exit discharge level must meet smokeproof enclosure standards per Section 1023.11.

question: Under the 2023 FBC, what is the maximum encroachment allowed by a door into a required means-of-egress width when the door is fully open?
gold_answer: When fully open, doors shall not reduce the required means-of-egress width by more than 7 inches (178 mm), and doors in any position shall not reduce the required width by more than one-half.

question: Which section of the Collier County Code of Ordinances adopts the Florida Building Code and sets out the County's local additions to it?
gold_answer: Section 22-26 (Chapter 22, Buildings and Building Regulations, Article II) adopts by reference the Florida Building Code, Eighth Edition (2023), and any amendments thereto, to be enforced in the unincorporated portions of Collier County, and contains the County's local additions, adjustments, amendments, and exemptions.

question: Which administrative section of the Florida Building Code did Collier County amend to allow approved digital construction documents to be kept at the work site?
gold_answer: Collier County amended FBC Section 107.3.1 (Approval of construction documents), based on FBC Section 107.3 and Section 553.74(4)(a) Florida Statutes, so that digital copies available at the site of work and open to inspection by the building official are also accepted.

question: To which Florida Building Code section did Collier County add local exemptions relating to permits for minor residential repairs and replacements?
gold_answer: Collier County added local permit exemptions under FBC 105.2 (Work exempt from permit), based on FBC Section 105.2.2 and Section 553.73(4)(a) Florida Statutes — e.g. flooring installation, painting/wallpaper, finish trim, interior door replacement, small drywall repair, gutters — plus a new Section 105.2.4 with exemptions for detached single-family residences.

question: Which prior ordinance did Collier County Ordinance 2023-64 repeal when it adopted the 2023 Florida Building Code?
gold_answer: Ordinance 2023-64 (Section Three: Repeal) repealed Ordinance No. 2020-48 in its entirety. Ordinance 2020-48, adopted December 8, 2020, had adopted the Florida Building Code, Seventh Edition (2020). Ordinance 2023-64 was effective December 31, 2023.

question: Which section of Collier County's flood ordinance (Code of Ordinances Chapter 62, Floods) governs requirements for buildings and structures in flood hazard areas?
gold_answer: Section 62-80 (Buildings and structures, Chapter 62 Article II Floodplain Management) governs buildings and structures in flood hazard areas: FBC-exempt buildings must follow ASCE 24 flood-resistant design, enclosed areas below elevated buildings are limited to parking/storage/building access, and detached accessory structures may sit below BFE only under specific conditions.

question: Which Collier County Code section establishes the duties and powers of the county floodplain administrator?
gold_answer: Section 62-73 (Chapter 62, Article II, Division 3) sets out the duties and powers of the Floodplain Administrator; it designates the County Manager or designee as the Floodplain Administrator, authorized to administer and enforce the flood ordinance.

question: Which Collier County Land Development Code section contains the dimensional standards table (Table 2.1) of minimum yard requirements for principal structures in base zoning districts?
gold_answer: LDC Section 4.02.01 (Dimensional Standards for Principal Uses in Base Zoning Districts) contains Table 2.1 - Table of Minimum Yard Requirements (Setbacks) for Base Zoning Districts, measured from the legal lot boundary except road right-of-way easements.

question: Under footnote (e) of Table 2.1 in Collier County LDC 4.02.01, what is the side yard rule for the Industrial (I) zoning district, expressed as a percentage of lot width?
gold_answer: The total of all side yard setbacks shall equal 20% of the lot width, with a maximum of 50 feet, and no side yard shall be less than 10 feet (alternative dimensions possible under a unified plan of development for commonly owned lots).

question: Which Collier County LDC section sets the specific standards for the location of accessory buildings and structures, such as pools and screen enclosures?
gold_answer: LDC Section 4.02.03 (Specific Standards for Location of Accessory Buildings and Structures) governs accessory structure setbacks and building separation. Accessory structures must be built simultaneously with or after the principal structure; in Rural Agricultural (A) and Estates (E) districts they must meet principal structure setbacks; other districts follow the section's dimensional standards table.

question: What is the minimum front yard setback for a principal structure in the Collier County Estates (E) zoning district?
gold_answer: 75 feet. Table 2.1 of LDC 4.02.01 sets the Estates (E) district minimum yards at 75 ft front, 30 ft side, and 75 ft rear.

question: What is the minimum lot area for the Estates (E) zoning district under Collier County LDC 4.02.01 Table 1?
gold_answer: 98,010 square feet (2.25 acres), with a minimum lot width of 150 linear feet, per Table 1 (Lot Design Requirements for Principal Uses in Base Zoning Districts) of LDC 4.02.01.

question: During sea turtle nesting season in Collier County, within how many feet of the nesting zone of a beach is it unlawful to construct a structure, add fill, mechanically clean the beach, or grade dirt without a permit?
gold_answer: Within 100 feet of the nesting zone. LDC 3.04.02(B)(5) (Species Specific Requirements - Sea Turtle Protection) makes it unlawful during nesting season to construct any structure, add fill, mechanically clean any beach, or grade dirt within 100 feet of the nesting zone of a beach where sea turtles nest or may nest, without obtaining a construction in sea turtle nesting area permit from the County Manager or designee.

question: Under Collier County's flood ordinance, to what elevation must mechanical, plumbing, and electrical systems be elevated for a detached accessory structure permitted below the base flood elevation?
gold_answer: To or above the base flood elevation plus one (1) foot. Sec. 62-80 permits detached accessory structures used only for parking or storage below the BFE provided (among other conditions) they resist flotation/collapse/lateral movement, use flood damage-resistant materials below BFE plus one foot, have mechanical, plumbing and electrical systems (including plumbing fixtures) elevated to or above BFE plus one foot, and the owner signs a Declaration of Land Restriction (non-conversion agreement).

question: Under Collier County's local FBC exemptions for detached single-family residences, what is the maximum value of non-structural repair work by a licensed contractor that is exempt from a building permit?
gold_answer: $1,500.00 in labor and materials. Collier County's added FBC Section 105.2.4 exempts repair work by licensed contractors that is not structural, does not change occupancy, does not affect life safety, and does not exceed $1,500 in value (the same $1,500 threshold applies per trade for electrical, plumbing, and mechanical repair work).

question: What is the maximum drywall repair area exempt from a building permit under Collier County's local amendments to FBC 105.2?
gold_answer: Drywall repair/replacement that is not fire-related and does not exceed two sheets of drywall (64 sq. ft.) is exempt from permit under Collier County's local additions to FBC 105.2.

question: Within how many days of the foundation inspection must the permit holder submit a spot survey of the building foundation, under Collier County's local amendment to FBC 110.3?
gold_answer: Within 10 calendar days of the foundation inspection, the permit holder must submit a spot survey depicting the building foundation to the authority having jurisdiction, prepared by or under the direct supervision of a Florida Registered land surveyor.

question: In addition to adopting the 2023 Florida Building Code, what wind-related local content did Collier County Ordinance 2023-64 incorporate as its Exhibit A?
gold_answer: Updated local Wind Zone Maps for Collier County (Categories 1 through 4), depicting wind speeds in 1-mph increments based on and interpolated from the 2023 FBC 8th Edition wind load maps, with the rule that the wind speed used for design shall be that of the highest adjacent line.

question: What is the maximum fine for violating the provisions of Collier County's building code ordinance, per Ordinance 2023-64?
gold_answer: A fine not to exceed $500.00, or imprisonment not to exceed 60 days in the county jail, or both, at the discretion of the court; each day of continued violation or noncompliance is a separate offense.

question: Under Collier County LDC 4.02.03, what is the maximum area an accessory building may occupy on a residentially zoned lot?
gold_answer: Accessory buildings may not occupy more than five (5) percent of the total lot area, or more than forty (40) percent of the building envelope, whichever is lesser - except that an accessory building under 500 square feet may be built if all yard and spacing requirements are met.

question: What is the minimum rear yard setback for a one- or two-family swimming pool and/or screen enclosure on a non-waterfront lot in Collier County?
gold_answer: 10 feet. The LDC 4.02.03 accessory structure table sets swimming pool and/or screen enclosure (one- and two-family) setbacks on non-waterfront/non-golf-course lots at SPS (same as principal structure) front and side, and 10 feet rear.
